snp4.13:2.1: “[The praise:] It’s such a small thing, not at all appeasing.
snp4.13:2.2: I speak of two fruits of dispute;
snp4.13:2.3: and seeing this, you shouldn’t dispute—
snp4.13:2.4: seeing the state where there’s no dispute as secure.
snp4.13:3.2: One who knows doesn’t enter into any conventions born of the run-of-the mill at all.
snp4.13:3.4: When he’s forming no predilection for what’s seen, for what’s heard, why would he get involved?
snp4.13:4.1: Those for whom habits are ultimate say that purity’s a matter of self-restraint.
snp4.13:4.2: Undertaking a practice, they devote themselves to it:
snp4.13:4.3: ‘Let’s train just in this, and then there would be purity.’
snp4.13:4.4: Those who say they are skilled are [thus] led on to becoming.
snp4.13:5.1: But if one of them falls from his habits or practice,
snp4.13:5.2: he trembles, having failed in his actions.
snp4.13:5.3: He hopes for, longs for, purity,
snp4.13:5.4: like a caravan leader lost far from home.
snp4.13:6.1: But one who’s abandoned habits & practices —all—
snp4.13:6.4: would live in kindness & peace, without taking up peace, detached.
snp4.13:7.2: or what’s seen, heard, or sensed,
snp4.13:7.3: they speak of purity through wandering further on through becoming & not-,
snp4.13:7.4: their craving not gone for becoming & not-.
snp4.13:8.1: For one who aspires has longings
snp4.13:8.3: But one who here has no passing away & arising:
snp4.13:8.4:
Why would he tremble?
For what would he long?”
snp4.13:9.2: is the very one others call ‘lowly.’
snp4.13:9.4: when all of these claim to be skilled?”
snp4.13:10.1: “They say their own teaching is perfect
snp4.13:10.2: while the doctrine of others is lowly.
snp4.13:10.4: each saying his agreed-on opinion is true.
snp4.13:11.1: If something, because of an opponent’s say-so, were lowly,
snp4.13:11.2: then none among teachings would be superlative,
snp4.13:11.3: for many say that another’s teaching’s inferior
snp4.13:12.1: If their worship of their teaching were true,
snp4.13:12.2: in line with the way they praise their own path,
snp4.13:12.3: then all doctrines would be true—
snp4.13:12.4: for purity’s theirs, according to each.
snp4.13:13.1: The brahman has nothing led by another,
snp4.13:13.2: when considering what’s grasped among doctrines.
snp4.13:13.3: Thus he has gone beyond disputes,
snp4.13:13.4: for he doesn’t regard as best the knowledge of a doctrine, any other doctrine.
snp4.13:14.1:
‘I know.
I see.
That’s just how it is!’—
snp4.13:14.2: Some believe purity’s in terms of view.
snp4.13:14.3: But even if a person has seen, what good does it do him?
snp4.13:14.4: Having slipped past, they speak of purity in connection with something or somebody else.
snp4.13:15.1: A person, in seeing, sees name-&-form.
snp4.13:15.2: Having seen, he’ll know only these things.
snp4.13:15.3: No matter if he’s seen little, a lot,
snp4.13:15.4: the skilled don’t say purity’s in connection with that.
snp4.13:16.1: A person entrenched in his teachings, preferring a theorized view, isn’t easy to discipline.
snp4.13:16.3: Whatever he depends on he describes it as lovely,
snp4.13:16.4: says that it’s purity, that there he saw truth.
snp4.13:17.1: The brahman, evaluating, doesn’t enter into a theory,
snp4.13:17.2: doesn’t follow views, isn’t tied even to knowledge.
snp4.13:17.3: And on knowing whatever’s conventional, commonplace,
snp4.13:17.4:
he remains equanimous:
‘That’s what others hold onto.’
snp4.13:18.1: Having untied the knots here in the world, the sage here in the world
snp4.13:18.2: doesn’t follow a faction when disputes have arisen.
snp4.13:18.3: At peace among those not at peace, he’s equanimous, doesn’t hold on:
snp4.13:19.1: Giving up old effluents, not forming new,
snp4.13:19.2: neither pursuing desire, nor entrenched in his teachings,
snp4.13:19.3: he’s totally released from viewpoints, enlightened.
snp4.13:19.4: He doesn’t adhere to the world, is without self-rebuke;
snp4.13:20.1: is enemy-free with regard to all things
snp4.13:20.3: His burden laid down, the sage totally released
snp4.13:20.4:
is improper ::
is theory-free hasn’t stopped ::
isn’t impassioned isn’t worth wanting ::
doesn’t desire,” the Blessed One said.
